zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon於1381年3月13日出生在法國布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易時，他的父親Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon44歲，母親Anne d'Auvergne23歲。他和Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne至少有3個兒子。他去世於1434年1月14日在法國奧維阿利也享年52歲，埋葬在法國奧維阿利也索維尼。

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourbon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon was born on 13 March 1381, in Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, his father, Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon, was 44 and his mother, Anne d'Auvergne, was 23. He had at least 3 sons with Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne. He died on 14 January 1434, in Allier, Auvergne, France, at the age of 52, and was buried in Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon est né le 13 mars 1381, à Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, son père, Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Anne d'Auvergne, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne. Il est décédé le 14 janvier 1434, à Allier, Auvergne, France, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon am 13. März 1381 in Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon 44 und seine Mutter Anne d'Auvergne 23. Er hatte mit Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 14. Januar 1434 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich und wurde in Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon è nato il il 13 marzo 1381 nel Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, il suo padre, Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Anne d'Auvergne, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne. Morì il 14 gennaio 1434, nel Allier, Auvergne, Francia, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbonが1381年3月13日にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeサoネ-エト-ロイアーで生まれた時，父Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbonは44歳，母Anne d'Auvergneは23歳であった。彼はMarie de Berry Duchess of Auvergneとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1434年1月14日にフランスアウバーグンアリールで52歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグンアリールソウヴィグニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon(이)가 1381년 3월 13일 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 사o니-트-로이르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne d'Auvergne(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1434년 1월 14일 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어 소우비그니에 묻혔다.

pt.13 March 1381–14 January 1434: 13 de março de 1381–14 de janeiro de 1434
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon nasceu em 13 de março de 1381, em Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, seu pai, Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Anne d'Auvergne, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne. Ele faleceu em 14 de janeiro de 1434, em Allier, Auvergne, França, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean de Bourbon I - Troisième Maison de Bourbon родился 13 марта 1381 г. в местности саoне-ет-лоир, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция, его отцу по имени Louis de Bourbon II le Bon, duc de Bourbon было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Anne d'Auvergne, 23. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne. Он умер 14 января 1434 г. в местности аллиир, аувиргн, Франция в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности соувигни, аллиир, аувиргн, Франция.

Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Marie de Berry Duchess of Auvergne. Murió el 14 de enero de 1434, en Allier, Auvergne, Francia, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Souvigny, Allier, Auvergne, Francia.
